page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64-based AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The server features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The underlying virtualization is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates solid multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0, both placing in the average tier. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0 and a weak rating in single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-threaded operations scale effectively, demonstrated by a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 64.62 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1894.03 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 173.34 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

Given the dedicated ARM64 architecture and the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, this instance is well-suited for workloads requiring high-speed local storage and balanced compute, such as database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It achieved 40.61 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 33.11 GB/sec in static web serving. The absence of x86-specific instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX limits its suitability for specialized vector math or heavy x86 legacy applications, but it offers a highly resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for native ARM64 deployments, containerized microservices, and medium-scale database hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by an AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network speed of 12 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0, though single-core performance is weaker. The instance excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and static web serving, achieving 40.61 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. Lacking x86 vector extensions, it is optimized for native ARM64 workloads, containerized applications, and high-speed local storage tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
64.0 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Strong multi-threaded performance and high-speed memory bandwidth.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and ARM64 native applications.
